summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture of an energetic April ’71 Pittsburgh show, highlighted by a scorching Other One, epic Lovelight segue and tight Pigpen‑driven jams.
review: The recording is a very crisp SBD source that lets every instrument shine, though occasional tape drops and missing sections keep it from being flawless. Musically the concert is a high‑water mark for spring ’71 – Pigpen’s bluesy grit, Phil’s booming bass, and tight group interplay make the second set especially memorable with a monster Other One, a seamless New Futures > Good Times Bad Times > Lovelight sequence and fiery Truckin’/Sugar Magnolia. Overall listeners rave about the performance despite a few glitches.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give 4‑5 stars and praise both the performance (standout jams, strong Pigpen, Phil, and tight group play) while noting only occasional recording glitches; the weighted average of show (70%) and recording (30%) quality lands in the good‑to‑great range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Exceptionally clear, crisp SBD with vocals and instruments up close; audience barely audible. Several reviewers note tape glitches, missing sections (e.g., half of H2H, Hard to Handle) and occasional drop‑outs that prevent an 'excellent' rating.

standout_songs: New Futures > Good Times Bad Times > Lovelight
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set starts a bit shaky but tightens by the second song; second set launches with Truckin' and builds into an intense Other One, seamless jams through NFA>GDTRFB>Lovellight, and finishes on high energy. The show feels like a solid, rock‑forward 1971 performance despite curfew constraints.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar is described as fired up, crisp, and driving especially on Hard to Handle, Loser, and the Other One; vocal moments noted as occasionally loose but energetic.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass is repeatedly praised – ‘in charge’, prominent in the mix, high‑gear playing that anchors the set.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob Weir’s rhythm guitar gets strong kudos for tightening up, driving Hard to Handle and Johnny B. Goode; his chord work noted as a highlight.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keyboards are mentioned only sparsely – some reviewers note little or no keyboards audible, while others simply accept the mix without comment.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann’s drumming is highlighted on the Other One and Lovelight with ‘white‑hot’ intensity; overall drum work described as solid.
